title: Before the Flood
story_html:
Mia and her cousin Sam were spending the weekend on their grandmother's small island. Mia always enjoyed these visits, especially Grandma's stories. Grandma was a true native of the place, and she knew every path and tree. Mia was talkative and loved asking questions, while clever Sam liked to fix things and figure out puzzles.

On Saturday morning, dark clouds rolled in. Grandma warned them that a flood might come if the rain did not stop. "We need to protect the house immediately," she said. Mia felt fearful, but she agreed to help.

The three of them worked fast. Sam wanted to guard the little boat by tying it to a post, while Mia's job was to move Grandma's old glass jars away from the window before they could break. Grandma, who could be a bit stubborn about her garden, refused to leave her plants outside and insisted on carrying them in herself.

Suddenly, the wind grew strong enough to shake the shutters, and the yard turned wet and muddy. The rain continued for an hour, painting the sky an ugly gray. Mia worried this would ruin her happy memory of summers there, once so popular with visitors who came to swim and fish.

Then, just as quickly as it began, the storm eased. Sam gave a small, almost bossy order — "Everyone check your own area!" — and they laughed because he sounded just like their teacher. Working together, they managed to complete every task on Grandma's list, solving each small problem as it came.

Grandma smiled and hugged them both, caring as always. Mia looked at her cousin and grandmother, and admired how calm they had stayed. It was not a perfect afternoon, but the three of them connected so well as a team that it was one Mia would never forget.

flood.def: Too much water in one place.

clever.def: Quick to understand or find smart solutions.

continue.def: To keep doing something without stopping.

wet.def: Covered in water or another liquid.

shake.def: To move quickly back and forth or up and down.

connect.def: To join or link two things together.

stubborn.def: Refusing to change your mind or the way you do things.

warn.def: To tell someone about a possible danger.

bossy.def: Liking to tell other people what to do.

caring.def: Feeling and showing concern for other people.

admire.def: To respect or think highly of someone or something.

agree.def: To have the same opinion as someone else.

popular.def: Liked or enjoyed by many people.

fearful.def: Feeling afraid or full of fear.

island.def: Land with water all around it.

memory.def: Something you remember from the past.

problem.def: A trouble that needs to be solved.

glass.def: A clear material, or a container made from it.

questions.qtext: In "Grandma warned them that a flood might come if the rain did not stop," what does warned mean?
options: Told them about a possible danger
Forgot to tell them something important
questions.explain: B — Grandma told the kids about the danger of a flood before it happened, which is what "warned" means.

questions.qtext: In "Sam wanted to guard the little boat by tying it to a post," what does guard mean?
options: To paint something a new color
questions.explain: C — guarding the boat means keeping it safe, which is why Sam tied it down before the storm.

questions.qtext: In "Grandma, who could be a bit stubborn about her garden, refused to leave her plants outside," what does stubborn mean?
options: Refusing to change your mind or the way you do things
questions.explain: A — Grandma insisted on doing things her own way and would not be talked out of it, which shows she is stubborn.

questions.qtext: Why did Mia, Sam, and their grandmother rush to work on Saturday morning?
options: They were getting ready for a party
A flood was coming and they needed to protect the house
They wanted to go fishing before it got dark
Visitors were arriving on the island soon
questions.explain: B — dark clouds rolled in, Grandma warned of a possible flood, and they worked quickly to protect the house.

questions.qtext: How did Mia feel about the afternoon by the end of the story?
options: She thought it was a boring, forgettable day
She was angry that Sam gave orders
She felt it was memorable because they worked so well together
She wished she had stayed home instead
questions.explain: C — even though it wasn't a perfect afternoon, Mia felt the three of them connected so well that she would never forget it.
